text:
I feel a sense of observation and introspection in these spaces. In the first image, I am positioned in a corridor, peering through glass at a group—likely children and an adult guide—engaged in an activity. I am separated from their world; I linger on the periphery, curious but isolated, reflecting on the boundaries between observer and participant. 

In the second image, I am surrounded by the language of innovation and research—R+D—inside what appears to be a museum or exhibition. The wall prompts me to think about progress, the blending of technology and humanity, and my own relationship to knowledge and discovery. Here, I feel both inspired and slightly overwhelmed by the abstract promises of development. In both scenes, I feel like a conscious observer, aware of the layers of meaning between inside and outside, engagement and reflection.
